task_completion.description: Verify that the GIMP application is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that an image is loaded into the GIMP workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image (likely a flower) is displayed in the main GIMP canvas.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Layers panel is visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layers dockable dialog/panel is open and visible in the GIMP interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the active layer has been duplicated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layers panel shows exactly two layers (e.g., the original layer and a duplicate like 'Background copy').

task_completion.description: Verify that the two-layer document is saved as an XCF file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Image' dialog is visible saving the file with an '.xcf' extension, or the GIMP window title bar displays an '.xcf' filename.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GIMP GUI rather than terminal commands to duplicate the layer.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the GIMP interface (e.g., menus, right-click context menus, or the Layers panel) rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: Verify the duplication occurred within the GIMP application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Layers panel clearly shows two distinct layer entries within a single GIMP document workspace, not just two separate image windows.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use command-line image manipulation tools to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows do not show commands like 'convert', 'magick', or python scripts creating the XCF file directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires opening a flower image in GIMP and duplicating its layer to create a two-layer document. The final expected output is a GIMP XCF file containing two layers (typically named 'Background' and a copy). Look for the GIMP interface, the loaded flower image, the Layers panel showing two layers, and evidence of the file being saved as an XCF document.
